display.text:
model: gemma-4-26b
task: generate_mujoco_xml

instructions: |
  Generate a valid MuJoCo XML model of a simple ideal pendulum using SI units.

  Coordinate system:
    +x = right
    +y = upward
    +z = out of page

  Pendulum definition:
    - Massless rod
    - Spherical bob
    - Single hinge joint

  Joint specifications:
    - Type: hinge
    - Location: [0, 1, 0]
    - Axis: [0, 0, 1]
    - Damping: 0
    - Friction: 0
    - No losses

  Rod:
    - Length: 0.2485 m
    - Mass: 0
    - Collision: disabled

  Bob:
    - Shape: sphere
    - Diameter: 0.1 m
    - Mass: 1.0 kg

  Environment:
    - Gravity: [0, -9.81, 0]

  Initial conditions:
    - Pendulum starts horizontal
    - Bob positioned along +x from hinge
    - Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical
    - Initial angular velocity: 0 rad/s

  MuJoCo requirements:
    - Enable energy computation
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Joint rotates only about z-axis
    - Use SI units

  Acceptance criteria:
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Rod length = 0.2485 m
    - Rod mass = 0 kg
    - Bob mass = 1 kg
    - Gravity = [0, -9.81, 0]
    - Initial angle = +90 degrees
    - Initial angular velocity = 0
    - Energy computation enabled

output_format:
  type: xml
  constraints:
    - Return only valid MuJoCo XML
    - No explanations
    - No additional text

state.title: YAML_creation/Temperature_Testing/V9_Temp_0.5/test01/ChatGPT-5.3-Instant_Pend.YAML

input.content:
<mujoco>
  <model>
    <compiler angle="degree" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
    <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" timestep="0.001"/>
    <asset>
      <texture name="grid" type="2d" builtin="checker" rgb1=".1 .2 .3" rgb2=".2 .3 .4" width="300" height="300"/>
      <material name="grid" texture="grid" texrepeat="1 1"/>
    </asset>
    <worldbody>
      <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
      <geom name="floor" type="plane" size="2 2 .1" material="grid"/>
      <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
        <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
        <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.01" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
        <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
          <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
        </body>
      </body>
    </worldbody>
  </model>
</mujoco>

<mujoco>
  <compiler angle="degree" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" timestep="0.001"/>
  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    <geom name="floor" type="plane" size="2 2 .1" rgba=".9 .9 .9 1"/>
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.01" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>
</mujoco>

input.command:
python3 -c '
import mujoco
import numpy as np

try:
    model = mujoco.MjModel.from_xml_path("pendulum.xml")
    data = mujoco.MjData(model)
    
    # Initial conditions
    # Pendulum starts horizontal: hinge is at (0,1,0), bob is at (0.2485, 1, 0)
    # Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical (vertical is usually along y or z depending on gravity)
    # Gravity is [0, -9.81, 0], so vertical is along y.
    # A 90 degree angle from vertical (downward) would be horizontal.
    # The joint axis is [0, 0, 1], so rotation is in the XY plane.
    # If the hinge is at 0 and the bob is at +x, the angle is 90 degrees if vertical is +y.
    
    # Set initial state
    # The joint is "hinge"
    joint_id = mujoco.mj_name2id(model, mujoco.mjtObj.mjOBJ_JOINT, "hinge")
    
    # The pendulum starts horizontal, bob positioned along +x from hinge.
    # The rod is from (0,0,0) to (0.2485, 0, 0) in local coordinates.
    # The hinge is at (0,1,0) in world.
    # If the angle is 0, the bob is at (0.2485, 1, 0) in world.
    # If vertical is (0, -1, 0), then 90 degrees from vertical is horizontal.
    # Let''s check the angle.
    
    data.qpos[joint_id] = np.pi / 2 # 90 degrees
    data.qvel[joint_id] = 0.0
    
    mujoco.mj_step(model, data)
    
    # Verification
    print(f"Gravity: {model.opt.gravity}")
    print(f"Hinge axis: {model.dof_xmat[joint_id][:3]}") # This is not quite right for axis, let''s use model.jnt_axis
    print(f"Hinge axis: {model.jnt_axis[joint_id]}")
    
    # Check mass
    bob_geom_id = mujoco.mj_name2id(model, mujoco.mjtObj.mjOBJ_GEOM, "bob_geom")
    print(f"Bob mass: {model.geom_mass[bob_geom_id]}")
    
    # Check rod mass (should be 0)
    rod_geom_id = mujoco.mj_name2id(model, mujoco.mjtObj.mjOBJ_GEOM, "rod")
    print(f"Rod mass: {model.geom_mass[rod_geom_id]}")

    # Energy computation
    # MuJoCo calculates energy automatically if we look at m than energy is not a direct property but can be derived
    # But we can check if it works by stepping
    print("Simulation step successful.")

except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
'

input.command:
python3 -c '
import mujoco
import numpy as np

try:
    model = mujoco.MjModel.from_xml_path("pendulum.xml")
    data = mujoco.MjData(model)
    
    joint_id = mujoco.mj_name2id(model, mujoco.mjtObj.mjOBJ_JOINT, "hinge")
    
    # Initial conditions: Pendulum starts horizontal (angle = pi/2 relative to vertical)
    # If gravity is [0, -9.81, 0], the vertical is along y.
    # The joint rotates about z.
    # A 90 degree angle from vertical (downward) would be horizontal.
    # In MuJoCo, joint position is the angle.
    data.qpos[joint_id] = np.pi / 2 
    data.qvel[joint_id] = 0.0
    
    mujoco.mj_step(model, data)
    
    # Verification
    print(f"Gravity: {model.opt.gravity}")
    
    # Check mass
    bob_geom_id = mujoco.mj_name2id(model, mujoco.mjtObj.mjOBJ_GEOM, "bob_geom")
    print(f"Bob mass: {model.geom_mass[bob_geom_id]}")
    
    # Check rod mass (should be 0)
    rod_geom_id = mujoco.mj_name2id(model, mujoco.mjtObj.mjOBJ_GEOM, "rod")
    print(f"Rod mass: {model.geom_mass[rod_geom_id]}")

    print("Simulation step successful.")

except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
'

try:
    model = mujoco.MjModel.from_xml_path("pendulum.xml")
    data = mujoco.MjData(model)
    
    joint_id = mujoco.mj_name2id(model, mujoco.mjtObj.mjOBJ_JOINT, "hinge")
    
    # Initial conditions: Pendulum starts horizontal (angle = pi/2 relative to vertical)
    # If gravity is [0, -9.81, 0], the vertical is along y.
    # The joint rotates about z.
    # A 90 degree angle from vertical (downward) would be horizontal.
    # In MuJoCo, joint position is the angle.
    data.qpos[joint_id] = np.pi / 2 
    data.qvel[joint_id] = 0.0

input.command:
python3 -c '
import mujoco
import numpy as np

try:
    model = mujoco.MjModel.from_xml_path("pendulum.xml")
    data = mujoco.MjData(model)
    
    joint_id = mujoco.mj_name2id(model, mujoco.mjtObj.mjOBJ_JOINT, "hinge")
    
    # Initial conditions
    data.qpos[joint_id] = np.pi / 2 
    data.qvel[joint_id] = 0.0
    
    mujoco.mj_step(model, data)
    
    # Verification
    print(f"Gravity: {model.opt.gravity}")
    
    # Check mass using body names
    bob_body_id = mujoco.mj_name2id(model, mujoco.mjtObj.mjOBJ_BODY, "bob")
    pendulum_body_id = mujoco.mj_name2id(model, mujoco.mjtObj.mjOBJ_BODY, "pendulum")
    
    print(f"Bob mass: {model.body_mass[bob_body_id]}")
    print(f"Rod mass: {model.body_mass[pendulum_body_id]}")

    print("Simulation step successful.")

except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
'
